questions.q: In the Ramayana, what kind of object is the Pushpaka Vimana?
options: A flying aerial chariot or palace
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is described in the Ramayana as a self-moving flying chariot or aerial palace.

questions.q: According to the Ramayana, which divine architect is said to have created the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana credits the divine architect Vishvakarma with crafting the Pushpaka Vimana.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, which god of wealth was the original owner of the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana originally belonged to Kubera, the god of wealth, before it was seized from him.

questions.q: Which demon-king of Lanka forcibly seized the Pushpaka Vimana from its original owner?
questions.explanation: Ravana, the rakshasa king of Lanka, seized the Pushpaka Vimana from Kubera by force.

questions.q: On which island kingdom did Ravana keep the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: Ravana kept the seized Pushpaka Vimana at his kingdom of Lanka.

questions.q: After Ravana's defeat, which prince of Ayodhya used the Pushpaka Vimana to return home?
questions.explanation: After Ravana's fall, Rama used the Pushpaka Vimana to travel back to Ayodhya.

questions.q: Whom did Rama bring with him aboard the Pushpaka Vimana on the journey back to Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama returned to Ayodhya aboard the Pushpaka Vimana together with Sita and his brother Lakshmana.

questions.q: How did the Pushpaka Vimana move, according to the Ramayana?
options: At the will of its rider
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is famed for moving at the will or thought of whoever commanded it.

questions.q: From which city did Rama begin his return flight to Ayodhya in the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: Rama set out in the Pushpaka Vimana from Lanka after Ravana's defeat to return to Ayodhya.

questions.q: The Sanskrit word 'vimana' in 'Pushpaka Vimana' broadly refers to what?
options: A royal throne fixed to the ground
A flying chariot or aerial vehicle
questions.explanation: 'Vimana' broadly denotes a flying chariot or aerial vehicle in Sanskrit literature.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, Ravana was the half-brother of which deity from whom he took the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: Kubera, the original owner of the Pushpaka Vimana, was Ravana's half-brother; both were sons of the sage Vishrava.

questions.q: After Rama reached Ayodhya, what did he do with the Pushpaka Vimana, according to the Ramayana?
options: Kept it locked in Ayodhya forever
questions.explanation: Having used it for his journey, Rama released the Pushpaka Vimana to return to its rightful owner, Kubera.

questions.q: The name 'Pushpaka' is linked to which Sanskrit word, reflecting the vimana's flower-like beauty?
questions.explanation: 'Pushpaka' derives from 'pushpa', meaning flower, evoking the chariot's flower-adorned splendor.

questions.q: In the Sundara Kanda of the Ramayana, which devotee of Rama beholds the magnificent Pushpaka Vimana while searching Ravana's Lanka for Sita?
questions.explanation: Hanuman marvels at the splendid Pushpaka Vimana during his reconnaissance of Lanka in the Sundara Kanda.

questions.q: A celebrated feature of the Pushpaka Vimana in the Ramayana is that it could do what?
options: Turn invisible to its own rider
Expand to hold any number of passengers without crowding
questions.explanation: The Ramayana describes the Pushpaka Vimana as ever spacious, expanding to accommodate any number of passengers.

questions.q: In which book (kanda) of Valmiki's Ramayana does Rama's return flight in the Pushpaka Vimana take place?
questions.explanation: Rama's victory over Ravana and his return aboard the Pushpaka Vimana are narrated in the Yuddha Kanda.

questions.q: In Hindu tradition, the deity Kubera who first owned the Pushpaka Vimana is the guardian of which direction?
questions.explanation: Kubera, the god of wealth and first owner of the vimana, is the guardian of the northern direction.

questions.q: On his return flight in the Pushpaka Vimana, Rama is said to have shown Sita sites of the war, including the bridge his army built across the sea. What is that bridge called?
questions.explanation: From the Pushpaka Vimana, Rama pointed out Rama Setu, the causeway his vanara army built to reach Lanka.

questions.q: The Pushpaka Vimana is a celestial wonder of Hindu epics. What is the closest English sense of 'vimana' in this context?
questions.explanation: In this epic context 'vimana' is best rendered as an aerial chariot or flying vehicle of the gods.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, who originally bestowed great boons and possessions upon Kubera, the first owner of the Pushpaka Vimana?
questions.explanation: Kubera attained his lordship of wealth and his treasures through the favor of Brahma in the Ramayana tradition.

questions.q: What did Rama choose to do with the Pushpaka Vimana so that it could resume serving its true master?
options: Honor Ravana's last wish to burn it
Leave it in Lanka at Hanuman's request
questions.explanation: Rama chose to return the Pushpaka Vimana to Kubera, restoring the vehicle to its rightful owner.

questions.q: The Pushpaka Vimana is classified in the card as which type of item in Indian Mythology?
questions.explanation: The Pushpaka Vimana is presented as a celestial artifact, a divine flying chariot.

questions.q: Both Kubera, the first owner of the Pushpaka Vimana, and Ravana, who seized it, were sons of which sage in the Ramayana?
questions.explanation: Kubera and Ravana were both sons of the sage Vishrava, which made them half-brothers.

questions.q: Vishrava, father of the vimana's owners, was himself the son of which sage, the grandfather of Ravana?
questions.explanation: Vishrava was the son of the sage Pulastya, making Pulastya the grandfather of both Kubera and Ravana.

questions.q: After dispossessing Kubera of Lanka and the Pushpaka Vimana, Ravana drove his half-brother to rule from which northern city?
questions.explanation: Dispossessed by Ravana, Kubera withdrew to rule his wealth from Alaka, his city in the Himalayas.

questions.q: When Rama flew to Ayodhya in the Pushpaka Vimana, the righteous brother of Ravana whom Rama had crowned king of Lanka was also honored. Who was he?
questions.explanation: Vibhishana, Ravana's virtuous brother, was made king of Lanka and is associated with Rama's triumphant return.

questions.q: During the return flight, Rama is said to have stopped near the hermitage of which sage so the travelers could pay respects before reaching Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: In the Yuddha Kanda, Rama halts at the hermitage of the sage Bharadvaja during the journey home in the Pushpaka Vimana.

questions.q: Whom did Rama send ahead from Bharadvaja's hermitage to carry news of his return to the waiting Bharata in Ayodhya?
questions.explanation: Rama dispatched Hanuman ahead to announce his return to Bharata before the Pushpaka Vimana reached Ayodhya.

questions.q: In the Ramayana, the Pushpaka Vimana is repeatedly likened in radiance to which celestial body?
questions.explanation: The Ramayana likens the brilliant Pushpaka Vimana to the radiance of the sun.

questions.q: Which festival, in popular observance, is traditionally associated with Rama's homecoming to Ayodhya at the end of the journey that included the Pushpaka Vimana flight?
questions.explanation: Popular tradition associates Rama's return to Ayodhya, completing the journey home, with the festival of Diwali.
